The town of Blackreach clung to the edge of the world, where the sea roared against the cliffs like a beast in pain. It was a place of salt-stained wind and forgotten secrets, where the fog rolled in like a living thing, swallowing the light and the lives of those who stayed too long.

Detective Elias Vane had seen his share of grim cases, but nothing prepared him for the disappearances. It started with a fisherman, then a schoolteacher, then a young mother. Each vanished without a trace, their homes left eerily untouched, as if they had simply stepped into the sea and never returned.

The locals spoke in hushed tones, their eyes darting when Vane asked questions. They called it the "Hollowing"—a legend passed down through generations. Some said the sea took them, others whispered of a curse that lured the unwary into the depths. But Vane didn’t believe in myths. He believed in evidence, in patterns, in the cold logic of crime.

He began his search at the shore, where the tide had carved deep grooves into the rock. He found the first clue—a set of footprints, half-buried in the sand, leading toward the sea. The prints were fresh, but the water had already begun to claim them. He followed the trail, deeper into the mist, where the air grew colder, the silence heavier.

Then he saw it—a figure standing at the water’s edge

Not just missing persons, but *vanishings*—no footprints, no signs, no bodies. Just empty houses, closed windows, and the sound of waves lapping against the shore like a mournful lullaby.

Detective Eleanor Voss arrived in the town at dusk, her boots crunching over the gravel path as she approached the old lighthouse. The air was thick with salt and something else—something ancient, like the sea itself was holding its breath. She had seen a lot in her years on the force, but this was different. This wasn’t a case. It was a *presence*.

All vanished without a trace. The townspeople whispered of curses, of old legends tied to the cliffs, of the sea taking its due.

**Title: *The Vanishing of Blackthorn Bay***
